categories.label: Items to Be WORN to In-Processing

items.name: ACU/MCU/OCP Uniform (Complete with Patches)
items.description: Full ACU, MCU, or OCP uniform worn to in-processing. Must be complete with patches.
items.notes: Worn on arrival. Must have patches.

items.name: Undershirt (Tan/Brown)
items.description: Military undershirt in tan or brown, worn to in-processing.
items.notes: Worn to in-processing.

items.name: Undershorts (Optional — Worn)
items.description: Undershorts worn to in-processing. Optional.
items.notes: Optional. Worn to in-processing.

items.name: Socks
items.description: Socks worn to in-processing.
items.notes: 1 pair worn to in-processing.

items.name: Patrol Cap (ACU/MCU/OCP Pattern)
items.description: Patrol cap in ACU, MCU, or OCP pattern. Worn to in-processing.
items.notes: Worn to in-processing.

items.name: Boots, Army Issue (Summer)
items.description: Army issue summer boots. Worn to in-processing.
items.notes: Worn to in-processing. 1 pair.

items.name: Military ID Card
items.description: Military ID card worn/carried to in-processing.
items.whyNeeded: Required for in-processing and access to facilities.

items.name: ID Tags (Dog Tags) on Standard Chain
items.description: Military dog tags on standard chain. No 550 cord or similar material.
items.notes: Must be on STANDARD CHAIN. No 550 cord or similar material.
items.whyNeeded: Worn at all times. Note 2 — belt (army issue non-stretch) will be used for specific tasks during survival training.
items.selectionTips: Standard issued chain only — no paracord or cord substitutes.

items.name: Cash (Minimum $50, Max $100, Small Denominations)
items.description: Cash in small denominations. Minimum $50.00, maximum $100.00.
items.notes: Minimum $50.00, not to exceed $100.00. Small denominations only.

items.name: Wristwatch (Inexpensive, No Compass or GPS)
items.description: Simple, inexpensive wristwatch with no compass or GPS.
items.notes: Inexpensive. Without compasses or GPS.
items.selectionTips: Bring an inexpensive watch — it may get damaged during training.

items.name: Pens (Black Ink)
items.description: Black ink pens.
items.notes: 2 pens, black ink.

items.name: Mechanical Pencil
items.description: Mechanical pencil.

items.name: Notebooks
items.description: Two notebooks, carried to in-processing.

items.name: Prescription Eyeglasses (Military or Civilian)
items.description: Prescription eyeglasses if required. Military issue or civilian. CONTACT LENSES WILL NOT BE WORN DURING ANY PORTION OF THE SERE COURSE.
items.notes: 2 pairs if required. CONTACT LENSES WILL NOT BE WORN DURING ANY PORTION OF THE SERE COURSE.
items.selectionTips: Contact lenses are absolutely prohibited for the entire SERE course.

items.name: LBE / LCE / FLC or Suspenders with Pistol Belt (No Items Tied Down)
items.description: Load bearing equipment — LBE, LCE, FLC, or suspenders with pistol belt. Worn to in-processing with no items tied down.
items.notes: NO items tied down when arriving.

items.name: Cover, Canteen 1 QT (Attached to LBE/FLC)
items.description: One-quart canteen covers attached to LBE, LCE, FLC, or suspenders.
items.notes: Attached to LBE, LCE, FLC or suspenders and pistol belt.

items.name: Canteen, 1 QT
items.description: One-quart military canteen, worn/carried to in-processing.

items.name: Cup, Canteen
items.description: Military canteen cup.

items.name: Compass, Lensatic (Army Issue, Attached to LBE/FLC)
items.description: Army issue lensatic compass attached to LBE, LCE, FLC, or suspenders.
items.notes: Attached to LBE, LCE, FLC or suspenders and pistol belt.
items.whyNeeded: Core skill for SERE land navigation.

items.name: Whistle
items.description: Military whistle, carried to in-processing.

items.name: Pouch, Ammo 5.56 (Attached to LBE/FLC)
items.description: 5.56mm ammo pouches attached to LBE, LCE, FLC, or suspenders.
items.notes: Attached to LBE, LCE, FLC or suspenders and pistol belt.

items.name: Fire Starting Device (Magnesium Bar or Ferro Rod)
items.description: Magnesium bar fire starting device or ferro rod.
items.notes: Magnesium Bar Fire Starting Device or Ferro Rod.
items.whyNeeded: Critical survival skill during SERE — fire starting is a core training element.
items.selectionTips: A ferro rod is more durable and reliable than a magnesium bar in wet conditions.

items.name: Safety Glasses (Clear Lens with Case — No Sunglasses)
items.description: Clear lens safety glasses with case. Sunglasses are NOT authorized.
items.notes: Clear lens only. With case. NO SUNGLASSES.

items.name: Knife — Pocket or Fixed Blade (Blade Under 7 Inches) or Multi-Tool
items.description: Pocket or fixed blade knife with blade under 7 inches, OR a multi-tool.
items.notes: Blade must be UNDER 7 inches. No machetes. Note 1 — no machetes or other knives with a blade over 7 inches.
items.selectionTips: Fixed blade preferred for survival use, but pocket or multi-tool authorized.

items.name: Sports Bra (Female Soldiers — Worn)
items.description: Sports bra designed for high intensity activities, IAW AR 670-1. FEMALE SOLDIERS ONLY. Worn to in-processing.
items.notes: FEMALE SOLDIERS ONLY. IAW AR 670-1. Designed for high intensity activities.

categories.label: Items to Be Packed in Rucksack

items.name: Field Pack, ALICE or MOLLE (Large with Frame)
items.description: Large ALICE or MOLLE field pack with frame.

items.name: Patrol Bag, Bivy Cover (GORE-TEX), and Stuff Sack
items.description: Patrol sleeping bag, GORE-TEX bivy cover, and stuff sack. All 3 systems together if issued.
items.notes: ALL 3 SYSTEMS TOGETHER if issued: Patrol Bag + Bivy Cover (Gortex) + Stuff Sack.

items.name: Flashlight with One Set of Extra Batteries (or Headlamp)
items.description: Flashlight or headlamp with one set of extra batteries.
items.notes: Headlamp may substitute. Include one extra set of batteries.

items.name: Bag, Wet Weather
items.description: Waterproof wet weather bag for protecting gear.

items.name: ACU/MCU/OCP Uniform (Full Sets)
items.description: Complete ACU, MCU, or OCP uniform sets packed in rucksack.
items.notes: 3 complete sets packed in ruck (in addition to the set worn).

items.name: Poncho, Wet Weather (If Issued)
items.description: Military wet weather poncho, if issued.
items.notes: If issued.

items.name: Tarp, Wet Weather — Subdued (Green/Brown/Black) or Civilian Tarp
items.description: Subdued wet weather tarp in green, brown, or black. If a military tarp is not issued, a civilian tarp is authorized in lieu.
items.notes: SUBDUED (GREEN/BROWN/BLACK) only if military. CIVILIAN TARP IS AUTHORIZED IN LIEU OF NOT BEING ISSUED A MILITARY TARP.
items.whyNeeded: Used for hasty shelters during evasion phases.
items.selectionTips: If you were not issued a military tarp, a civilian tarp in subdued colors is fully authorized.

items.name: Liner, Poncho (Woobie)
items.description: Military poncho liner.

items.name: Boots, Army Issue Summer (Additional Pair)
items.description: Additional pair of summer boots packed in rucksack (separate from the pair worn).
items.notes: Additional to the pair worn. 1 pair.
items.whyNeeded: Allows boot rotation to manage blisters during extended evasion.

items.name: Socks, Wool, Cushioned Sole
items.description: Wool socks with cushioned sole.
items.notes: 6 pairs.

items.name: Undershirts (Tan/Brown)
items.description: Military undershirts in tan or brown, packed in rucksack.
items.notes: 4 total (in addition to the one worn).

items.name: Sports Bras (Female Soldiers — Packed)
items.description: Sports bras designed for high intensity activities. FEMALE SOLDIERS ONLY.
items.notes: FEMALE SOLDIERS ONLY. 4 total (in addition to the one worn). IAW AR 670-1.

items.name: Undershorts
items.description: Undershorts packed in rucksack.
items.notes: 4 total (in addition to any worn).

items.name: Towels
items.description: Standard towels packed in rucksack.

items.name: Gloves, Leather Work (or Army Issue Black Leather)
items.description: Leather work gloves or Army issue black leather gloves.
items.notes: Leather work gloves or Army Issue black leather gloves.

items.name: Wet Weather Top, GORE-TEX
items.description: GORE-TEX wet weather top.

items.name: Wet Weather Bottom, GORE-TEX
items.description: GORE-TEX wet weather trousers.

items.name: SPEARS Top (Polypro Authorized)
items.description: SPEARS cold weather top. Polypro may substitute.
items.notes: Polypro authorized as substitute.

items.name: Personal Hygiene Items (19-Day Supply)
items.description: All personal hygiene supplies for 19 days. Females see Note 4 (pads only — no tampons).
items.notes: 19-day supply. NOTE 4: Female soldiers will need personal hygiene supplies for menstrual cycles in the form of PADS ONLY (no tampons) to cover a week timeframe. Other forms of hygiene supplies are not authorized.
items.selectionTips: Include soap, toothbrush/toothpaste, razors, shaving cream, etc. for the full 19 days.

items.name: 550 Cord (Minimum 20 Feet)
items.description: 550 paracord, minimum 20 feet.
items.notes: Minimum 20 feet.
items.whyNeeded: Used for shelter construction, gear repair, and improvised equipment during survival training.

items.name: Bungee Cords
items.description: Bungee cords for securing gear.

items.name: Protractor
items.description: Military protractor for land navigation.

items.name: Map Markers
items.description: Map markers (alcohol-based) for overlays.
items.notes: 1 set.

items.name: Bobby Pins — Stay Right Brand (Model/Style 1352)
items.description: Bobby pins, specifically Stay Right brand, model/style 1352.
items.notes: STAY RIGHT BRAND, model/style 1352 specifically. 1 set.
items.whyNeeded: Used during specific SERE training tasks.
items.selectionTips: Must be the Stay Right brand, model/style 1352 — not a generic substitute.

items.name: Sewing Kit
items.description: Small sewing kit for field repairs.

items.name: Drinking Bottle (32 oz, Large Opening, Not Glass)
items.description: 32-ounce drinking bottle with large opening. Not glass.
items.notes: 32oz, large opening, NOT glass.

items.name: Bug Repellent
items.description: Insect repellent.

items.name: Baby Wipes
items.description: Baby wipes for field hygiene.
items.notes: 1 pack.

items.name: Shirt (Civilian Roughs / Hiking Attire)
items.description: Civilian shirt in rugged outdoor / hiking style.
items.notes: Should be seasonal and rugged. You will receive no reimbursement for damage. Think Tractor Supply type apparel.
items.selectionTips: Old, inexpensive outdoor clothing. Expect damage.

items.name: Pants (Civilian Roughs / Hiking Attire)
items.description: Civilian pants in rugged outdoor / hiking style.
items.notes: Should be seasonal and rugged. Old or inexpensive — expect damage.

items.name: Jacket (Civilian, Inclement Weather)
items.description: Civilian inclement weather jacket.
items.notes: Old or inexpensive — expect damage.

items.name: Baseball Style Cap (Optional)
items.description: Civilian baseball-style cap.
items.notes: Optional.

items.name: Carabiner Clip
items.description: Standard carabiner clip.

categories.label: Items to Be Packed in Duffel Bag

items.name: Bag, Barracks
items.description: Military barracks/laundry bag.

items.name: Shower Shoes
items.description: Shower shoes / flip-flops.

items.name: Army Physical Fitness Uniform (Summer — Shorts and Shirt)
items.description: Army PT uniform (summer set: shorts and shirt) or service equivalent.
items.notes: Or service equivalent.

items.name: Running Shoes
items.description: Running shoes for PT.

items.name: Socks, White Cotton
items.description: White cotton PT socks.
items.notes: 2 pairs.

items.name: Combination Lock
items.description: Combination lock for securing personal items.

items.name: Duffel Bag (or Aviators Kit Bag)
items.description: Two duffel bags or aviators kit bags for packing gear.

items.name: Fleece Jacket, Military Issue
items.description: Military issue fleece jacket. Worn or packed in rucksack. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Worn or in ruck sack. Required 01 October through 30 April.

items.name: Boots, Army Issue Cold Weather
items.description: Army issue cold weather boots. Worn or in rucksack. Replaces one set of summer boots. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Worn or in ruck sack. Replaces 1 set of summer boots. Required 01 October through 30 April.

items.name: Cold Weather Socks (Optional)
items.description: Cold weather socks in rucksack. Optional. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Optional. 4 pairs. In ruck sack. 01 Oct – 30 Apr.

items.name: APFU Jacket
items.description: Army Physical Fitness Uniform (APFU) jacket. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Required 01 October through 30 April.

items.name: APFU Pants
items.description: Army Physical Fitness Uniform (APFU) pants. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Required 01 October through 30 April.

items.name: Watch Cap (Black, ACU Green, or Brown)
items.description: Watch cap in black, ACU green, or brown. Worn or in rucksack. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Black, ACU green, or brown. Worn or in ruck sack. 01 Oct – 30 Apr.

items.name: Neck Gaiter
items.description: Cold weather neck gaiter. Worn or in rucksack. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Worn or in ruck sack. 01 Oct – 30 Apr.

items.name: Balaclava (Optional)
items.description: Cold weather balaclava. Optional. Worn or in rucksack. 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Optional. Worn or in ruck sack. 01 Oct – 30 Apr.

items.name: SPEARS Top (or Polypro Substitute)
items.description: SPEARS cold weather top or polypro substitute. Worn or in rucksack. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Or May Substitute With Polypro. Worn or in ruck sack. 01 Oct – 30 Apr.

items.name: SPEARS Bottom (or Polypro Substitute)
items.description: SPEARS cold weather bottom or polypro substitute. Worn or in rucksack. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Or May Substitute With Polypro. Worn or in ruck sack. 01 Oct – 30 Apr.

items.name: Patrol Bag, Intermediate Cold Weather Bag, Bivy Cover (GORE-TEX), and Stuff Sack
items.description: Complete winter sleep system: Intermediate Cold Weather Bag + Bivy Cover (GORE-TEX) + Stuff Sack. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Complete system required for winter classes (01 Oct – 30 Apr).

items.name: Cold Weather Gloves, Army Issue
items.description: Army issue cold weather gloves. Required 01 Oct – 30 Apr.
items.notes: Army Issue. 01 Oct – 30 Apr.

items.name: Pocket Size Survival Kit
items.description: Small personal survival kit.

items.name: Sleeping Mat (Military or Civilian)
items.description: Sleeping mat or pad, military or civilian.

items.name: Field Jacket Liner
items.description: Military field jacket liner.

items.name: Tobacco Products
items.description: Tobacco products.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Tent Stakes
items.description: Tent stakes for shelter construction.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Sewing Kit
items.description: Additional sewing kit (one is already mandatory in the ruck).

items.name: Reading Material (Unauthorized Types)
items.description: Reading material is prohibited EXCEPT: Religious texts, POW literature, Survival literature, Ranger Handbook, and Tactical Books.
items.notes: EXCEPTION: Religious writings, POW literature, Survival literature, Ranger Handbook, and Tactical Books are authorized.

items.name: Audio/Visual Electronics (Radio, Camera, iPod, Camcorder, Pager)
items.description: All audio/visual electronics prohibited. Cell phones will be collected day one and returned at out-processing.
items.notes: Cell phones will be collected on day 1 and returned at out-processing.

items.name: Camp Stoves or Heat Tablets
items.description: Camp stoves and heat tablets of any kind are prohibited.

items.name: Food Items
items.description: Any food items are prohibited.

items.name: Contact Lenses
items.description: Contact lenses will not be worn during ANY portion of the SERE course.
items.notes: Prescription eyeglasses (military or civilian) must be worn instead.

items.name: E-Tool (Entrenching Tool)
items.description: Entrenching tool (e-tool) is prohibited.

items.name: Saws
items.description: Saws of any kind are prohibited.

items.name: Tents, Hammocks, Civilian Shelter, or Tarp
items.description: Tents, hammocks, civilian shelters, and tarps are prohibited.

items.name: Non-Prescribed Medications
items.description: All non-prescribed medications are prohibited. All prescribed medications will be reviewed by the SERE medic.
items.notes: All prescribed medications will be reviewed by the SERE medic.

items.name: Dietary Supplements (Ephedrine, Creatine, etc.)
items.description: No dietary supplements authorized. Prohibited within 30 days prior to training. Specifically prohibited: (a) Ephedrine Sulfate (Ephedra, Ephedra Sinica, Ma-Huang); (b) Synthetic Creatine (Creatine Monohydrate, Citrate, Phosphate, or Monohydrate Salts).
items.notes: Students are prohibited from consuming or possessing any form of the following within 30 days prior to training: (a) Ephedrine Sulfate / Ephedra / Ma-Huang; (b) Synthetic Creatine Monohydrate (Citrate, Phosphate, Monohydrate Salts).

items.name: Machetes or Knives with Blade Over 7 Inches
items.description: No machetes or other knives with a blade over 7 inches. See Note 1.
